name: Ochsenschwanzsuppe (Ox Tail Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4-quart Dutch Oven, brown ox tails and onion in hot oil for several minutes.
2. Add water, salt, and peppercorns; cover and simmer uncovered for about 2 hours.
3. Uncover and continue to simmer for an additional 3 hours until the meat is tender.
4. Stir in parsley, carrots, celery, bay leaf, tomatoes, and thyme. Continue simmering for 30 minutes longer or until vegetables are tender.
5. Strain stock into a bowl, discard solids, and refrigerate for at least one hour.
6. In a blender, puree the meat and vegetables. Return purée to the cooled broth.
7. Skim fat from the top of the stock and reheat gently.
8. In a large dry frying pan, brown flour over high heat until golden brown.
9. Cool slightly, then gradually add butter or margarine while stirring.
10. Slowly incorporate the reserved stock and vegetables, adjusting seasoning as needed.
11. Add Madeira just before serving to enhance flavor.
